text:
News To Me

Edition date: 2026-05-02
Generated: 2026-05-02T14:15:10+00:00
Version: df9f41c1
Read online: https://news-to-n30b3vsid-bhargavs-projects-cfd6c9a5.vercel.app

TLDR
- US Supreme Court ruling reshapes congressional redistricting battle: Republicans and Democrats are fighting over new congressional district maps that could shift House power before 2026 midterms. Texas approved a map boosting Republican seats while California flipped its map to favor Democrats. With the House split 217-212, both parties are racing to lock in favorable lines.
- India sees 4,300% surge in digital payment fraud; RBI proposes one-hour transaction delays: Over 2.5 million Indians lost $2.5 billion to digital fraud in 2025, a 4,300% increase since 2021. The RBI proposed mandatory one-hour delays on transfers and extra authentication for elderly users. Critics question whether lagged payments would undermine India's fast payment infrastructure that makes UPI so popular.
- Seven killed as hospital wall collapses during Bengaluru hailstorm: A compound wall at Bowring Hospital in central Bengaluru collapsed during heavy rains, killing seven people including two children. Most victims were street vendors who had taken shelter from the storm. Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced Rs 5 lakh compensation and ordered an inquiry, with the opposition calling it a result of 'civic apathy.'
- Ranchi teen arrested for murdering mother with boyfriend's help: Police in Ranchi exhumed the body of 50-year-old Nahid Parveen after her family raised suspicions about a reported bathroom fall. Her 17-year-old adopted daughter allegedly killed her with help from her boyfriend, possibly over disputes about money transfers the mother opposed. An FIR has been registered under the new Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.
- Bengaluru KAS officer arrested accepting Rs 15 lakh bribe: Lokayukta police arrested Nagaprashanth, a Karnataka Administrative Service officer, and his driver while accepting a Rs 15 lakh bribe in Bengaluru. The officer allegedly demanded Rs 30 lakh to pass a favorable order in a land acquisition case. A case has been registered under the Prevention of Corruption Act.

Sections included:
- News regions: bangalore, karnataka, india, us, world
- Biz/Tech articles: 5
- Growth: Building a Marriage That Works Across Two Worlds: A Practical Guide for Young Professionals
- Knowledge: The Secret to Samsung's Family Dynasty: How One Korean Clan Has Controlled a Global Empire for Decades
- Riddle: I have cities, but no houses. I have mountains, but no trees. I have water, but no fish. What am I?

Edition: 2026-05-02  |  Generated: 2026-05-02T14:15:10+00:00  |  Version: df9f41c1

Read Full Edition →

TLDR

  • US Supreme Court ruling reshapes congressional redistricting battle
    Republicans and Democrats are fighting over new congressional district maps that could shift House power before 2026 midterms. Texas approved a map boosting Republican seats while California flipped its map to favor Democrats. With the House split 217-212, both parties are racing to lock in favorable lines.
  • India sees 4,300% surge in digital payment fraud; RBI proposes one-hour transaction delays
    Over 2.5 million Indians lost $2.5 billion to digital fraud in 2025, a 4,300% increase since 2021. The RBI proposed mandatory one-hour delays on transfers and extra authentication for elderly users. Critics question whether lagged payments would undermine India's fast payment infrastructure that makes UPI so popular.
  • Seven killed as hospital wall collapses during Bengaluru hailstorm
    A compound wall at Bowring Hospital in central Bengaluru collapsed during heavy rains, killing seven people including two children. Most victims were street vendors who had taken shelter from the storm. Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced Rs 5 lakh compensation and ordered an inquiry, with the opposition calling it a result of 'civic apathy.'
  • Ranchi teen arrested for murdering mother with boyfriend's help
    Police in Ranchi exhumed the body of 50-year-old Nahid Parveen after her family raised suspicions about a reported bathroom fall. Her 17-year-old adopted daughter allegedly killed her with help from her boyfriend, possibly over disputes about money transfers the mother opposed. An FIR has been registered under the new Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.
  • Bengaluru KAS officer arrested accepting Rs 15 lakh bribe
    Lokayukta police arrested Nagaprashanth, a Karnataka Administrative Service officer, and his driver while accepting a Rs 15 lakh bribe in Bengaluru. The officer allegedly demanded Rs 30 lakh to pass a favorable order in a land acquisition case. A case has been registered under the Prevention of Corruption Act.
